mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding supply and corporate earnings growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month increase is associated with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% indicates labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns exceeds 50% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Low real interest rates (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) reduce the discount rate on equity cash flows, stimulating investment and lifting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real rate signal remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the composite signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals deepening residential investment contraction, which depresses economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: During periods of HOUST YoY decline > 10%, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales reflect strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the short term of one week.
failure_condition: The monthly change in RSAFS has zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5%, it indicates extreme monetary accommodation in response to severe financial stress, which typically coincides with further equity market declines.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.5%, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in nonfarm payroll employment and retail sales signal synchronized weakening in the labor market and consumer demand, producing a powerful negative signal for forward equity returns.
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline month-over-month, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns exceeds 50% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI growth above 3% year-over-year), rising industrial production exacerbates overheating concerns and accelerates monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: In periods with CPI year-over-year growth above 3%, a rise in INDPRO is not associated with negative 60-day forward returns in a statistically significant manner (t-test p > 0.05).
